## [00:00 - 02:59]

Hi, this is Don Crowther. Let's talk about the replay promo part of your webinar sales funnel. So if let's go back and just put you in place of where this happens in the in the system. So you're mailing to your list or you've got external traffic coming in or you got affiliates emails coming into a webinar registration page. From there, they go they get in an audience promo. They attend the webinar. If they don't didn't buy They then see this replay promo where we're promoting people to watch the replay of the webinar. And by the way, there's one more process in this whole thing is if they say no to the webinar depends on whether they're mailing to your list They come from your list, but in other words, there's multiple ways where we get them to this replay promo If it's external traffic or affiliate email, we're getting them in. We're injecting them in if they say no to the webinar registration We're re-inject injecting them into the campaign at the replay promo stage So this is a very important stage because it determines everything that happens after that So your mission here is to get them to watch the recording of the webinar And if not, you want them to at least be still open to considering your offer So in other words, you don't want to burn bridges at this point You still want them to be very positive about your offer because again Just like there's 70% of the people won't watch Won't even attend a webinar There's about 80% of the people that won't ever watch the replay of the webinar So we still don't want to tick them off, but we do want to get that 20% who will because every little bit helps So here's how the replay promo works Basically, this is two to four emails Inviting them to watch the replay of your webinar. Let me take you through each one of those emails email one Is basically watch the video Watch the video of the replay. You're sending this as soon as possible after the webinar So if you were using a service that automatically records and encodes your webinar You can usually within an hour after the webinar have a recording of that video of that webinar Be able to slap that upon a slap it up on a page I'll talk about what that page should look like in a separate video and be able to email that out Personally, I write that email before I ever hold the webinar So that everything's ready. I've created a page All I had to do is get the video to drop in on the on the landing page And the email's all done. All I had to do is once I've got that email Once I've got that video on the landing page Then I just press send and send this out

## [03:00 - 06:02]

And the focus here is that you're talking about something that you talked about in the webinar So it's something amazing that you taught in that webinar that they're definitely going to want to see And it also includes what else they're going to learn from watching the replay And most of the cases you're talking what basically you're you're you're talking about the stuff you marketed Used to mark the webinar in the first place Now you're saying hey, it's all done. You can go watch it But I like to have this be at least slightly different I like to point out something new that they haven't seen before So that they'll be willing to come and watch the replay Email number two is what I call timestamps from the webinar So this is sent the next morning after the webinar and it contains three key points from the webinar Including the timestamps So what you're doing is you're saying hey, I taught this really cool thing At five minutes and 25 seconds into the webinar go here to watch the webinar And I'll put three of those into that so three different things that I taught with three different timestamps So that they can look at it and say well, I'm not interested in that one But I'm very interested in that one They click on it go to 13 minutes and 26 seconds and watch that portion of the webinar And what oftentimes happens is if I can get them to go in and watch at 13 minutes and 26 seconds They're going to watch a whole lot more than just that point. They might go back to the beginning They might watch from that point all the way to the end whatever works. I'll take it Okay, so I'm sending so the the key point of this Is that you're putting in these timestamps To show people hey You can skip all the other stuff if you want to and just go to these parts and get huge value That's what they think but then oftentimes they stay and watch a whole bunch more I also in this video include testimonials from the webinar attendees And a webinar expiration notification. I let them know. ## [06:02 - 09:06]

all right, the third email is You're talking about the bonus That you gave in the webinar the attendee bonus So it all depends on whether you're trying to How tight your cart open period is? but You're either sending this the afternoon of the day after the webinar or the next morning after that And your focus here is to get them to download the attendee bonus from the webinar So in the webinar you gave away this attendee bonus this really cool thing to get them to attend the webinar Well as you're sending out this email you're talking about that bonus and why they want to Want to download it and get it and then you're also including here some additional timestamps From the webinar some things you didn't mention in the previous email You're talking about some additional timestamps and you can also include a slide Download so you can give them the opportunity to download the slides also Now you'll do that if you if you choose to do this You that slide download link is actually on the webinar replay play page So they go there They start watching the webinar and then they get the opportunity to download the slides as well as the attendee bonus on that page Now optional You can replace email number three with a replay report And i'll talk about that in a separate in a separate video talking about the replay report strategy All right email number four Is sent day three after the webinar and the focus here is hey the replay is going away If you want to watch this come watch it now And it includes a summary of the things that that they're learning as well as the attendee bonus And i want to note here this email is actually sent after the sales page promo begins so It's the sales page promo starts after email number three But then this still happens later on too. So it's it's overlapping with the sales page promo Remember you're sending these emails to different audiences So you may want to address them differently in the text of your emails. Let me show you how you do that So Basically, we've got four audience segments that have that have happened at this point We got the first group who attended and watched to the end of the webinar We've got a second group who attended, but they left before your offer You've got a third group who registered, but they didn't attend

## [09:08 - 12:09]

And you got a fourth group who didn't register And just in case you're wondering how does this fit with those three audiences you talked about before? well Remember this chart with the showing the external traffic and the affiliate email and the opt-in delivery down here Well, those two groups They're included In the did not register for the webinar group So let's talk about How we would structure i'll just take example of email one At this watch the video watch the video and let's talk about how you would structure your email to each of these four different audiences So the first group is those who attended and watched to the end So what you're saying in the email to them is hey, thanks for attending multiple people asked for the recording. So here it is Then there is the group who attended but they left before the offer So here you're saying thanks for attending. Hey, I notice you had to leave before the end And so here's the recording and here's some things that you may have missed Now some people hesitate because they say that sounds a little creepy, but you know what? I like to actually acknowledge to them. Hey, you left before the end of the webinar You missed some good stuff if you do it without getting creepy you can pull that one off All right, the third group is the group who registered, but they didn't attend So here i'm saying sorry you missed the webinar. Here's what you missed. Here's a copy of the other recording The fourth group is the people who didn't register So I recently recorded a webinar about the subject of your webinar Here's what you missed and here's a copy of the recording. ## [12:10 - 13:12]

If your webinar converted at less than 20 to 25 You may want to only send two of these emails So i'm only going to send four emails out about the replay if my replay really did its job and did a good job Of converting the people who are on the webinar itself So that's the replay. Basically, you're going through a series of four two to four emails Where you are sending people to the replay to the recording Of the webinar now. Oh one other side note There are some Webinar packages that basically enable you to recreate the feeling of a webinar Those are designed for an evergreen webinar kind of setup That's not what you send these people to in this case. You're sending them to an actual recording of the webinar without the comments Streaming in and everything else like that. It's just a recording of the webinar a classic webinar replay
